title: Broadcom Q1 FY 2026: XPU Momentum Drives Strong Earnings
text: Broadcom delivered Q1 FY 2026 results with revenue of $19.31 billion (up 29% YoY), semiconductor solutions surged 52% to $12.5 billion. AI revenue reached $8.4 billion (106% YoY growth). CEO Hock Tan stated Q1 AI revenue grew 106% above forecast. Company highlighted acceleration of custom XPU programs across growing customer base, expanding from five to six named customers. AI networking revenue increasingly significant portion of semiconductor sales. Q1 represented step-up quarter with Q2 anticipated further growth. Company highlighted Tomahawk 6 switching technology and SerDes advancements as competitive advantages. VMware Cloud Foundation serves as AI data center abstraction layer. Management guided Q2 FY 2026 revenue approximately $22.0 billion (47% YoY), well above $20.5 billion consensus. Company signaled line of sight to AI chip revenue exceeding $100 billion in FY 2027.
